assertions.title: Center square "Gets nerdsniped" is markable like any other square

steps.expected: Board renders with center square unmarked
steps.observed: Center square 'Gets nerdsniped' rendered with aria-pressed="false", no checkmark, plain background
Square marked: ✓ prefix appeared, aria-pressed="true", background changed to bg-[var(--lagoon)]/20
Square unmarked: ✓ removed, aria-pressed="false"

steps.action: Mark 6 squares (e1, e3, e5, e13, e20, e25)
steps.observed: 6 squares marked: Reviews a viewer's PR, Mentions living in San Francisco, Roasts a tech startup, Gets nerdsniped, Gets emotional about open source, Rants about TypeScript for 5+ minutes

steps.expected: All 6 squares still marked after refresh
steps.observed: All 6 squares still marked with identical state after refresh

evidence.consoleErrors: Hydration mismatch warnings on game page (SSR renders all squares unmarked, client hydrates with sessionStorage marks). Functional behavior is correct — React re-renders with correct state.

steps.action: Set up history: navigate to / then to /game?seed=testpersist, mark 6 squares
steps.observed: 6 squares marked (marks persisted from sessionStorage even after navigation)
Navigated to http://localhost:3000/

steps.expected: Return to game page with all marks preserved
steps.observed: Returned to http://localhost:3000/game?seed=testpersist with all 6 squares still marked

assertions.title: Marking does not alter the URL seed

steps.action: Capture URL before clicking a square
steps.observed: URL is http://localhost:3000/game?seed=testpersist

steps.action: Click square e3 to mark it
steps.observed: Square marked, URL still http://localhost:3000/game?seed=testpersist
URL is http://localhost:3000/game?seed=testpersist — identical

steps.action: Inspect unmarked square (e2) styles at rest
steps.observed: background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.74) — white/transparent
background: rgba(79, 184, 178, 0.12) — teal/lagoon tinted, plus the CSS class includes hover:bg-[var(--lagoon)]/30 and hover:border-[var(--lagoon-deep)]

steps.action: On game page with 6 marked squares, press Back
steps.observed: Navigated to http://localhost:3000/

steps.expected: Return to game page with all marks preserved
steps.observed: All 6 squares still marked: Reviews a viewer's PR, Mentions living in San Francisco, Roasts a tech startup, Gets nerdsniped, Gets emotional about open source, Rants about TypeScript for 5+ minutes

evidence.consoleErrors: Hydration mismatch warnings (SSR vs client state)

steps.observed: 7 squares marked: Reviews a viewer's PR, Mentions living in San Francisco, Roasts a tech startup, Goes on an extended skateboard tangent, Gets nerdsniped, Gets emotional about open source, Rants about TypeScript for 5+ minutes

steps.expected: All 7 squares still marked after hard refresh
steps.observed: All 7 squares still marked with identical state

evidence.consoleErrors: Hydration mismatch warnings (SSR vs client state)

steps.action: Open /game?seed=sametest in Tab A (session 4564b48db884__smpers), mark 3 squares (e1, e5, e13)
steps.observed: 3 squares marked: Gets excited about a new framework then abandons it, Leaks his email / API key, Gets nerdsniped

steps.expected: Same board layout, all squares unmarked
steps.observed: Same board layout (verified by comparing square text arrays), 0 squares marked (aria-pressed=true array is empty)

steps.action: Mark a square in Tab B (e2)
steps.observed: Tab A still shows only the original 3 marked squares — Tab B's mark did not affect Tab A

frictions.description: Session started directly on game page — no prior history for back navigation test (VAL-SM-009). Had to navigate to landing page first then game page to build proper browser history.
frictions.resolution: Navigated to / first, then to /game?seed=testpersist to establish history before testing back/forward

frictions.description: Hydration mismatch warnings appear when page loads with previously marked squares in sessionStorage. Server renders all squares as unmarked, client hydrates with marked state from sessionStorage, causing React hydration mismatch.
frictions.resolution: Functional behavior is correct (React re-renders client-side), but the SSR markup doesn't account for sessionStorage state. This is a cosmetic/warning issue, not a functional bug.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: all 8 passed. Hydration mismatch warnings noted on game pages with persisted marks (SSR renders unmarked, client hydrates with sessionStorage marks), but functional behavior is correct for all persistence and marking features.
